questions.solution: A - should be 'She lost not only'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: innovation / for / key / is / growth / economic
options: Innovation is key for economic growth
Innovation is key for economic growth
Economic growth is key for innovation
For economic growth innovation is key
Key for growth economic is innovation
questions.solution: Innovation is key for economic growth

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Grinding — force and temperature:
options: High specific energy (10-20× turning). Most energy goes into heat → thermal damage
questions.solution: Grinding: high MRR per grit. Wheel loading/dulling → burn. Use coolant (soluble oil).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The scavenging process in 2-stroke engines removes:
options: Exhaust gases and admits fresh charge
questions.solution: Scavenging: replace exhaust with fresh charge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chip types:
options: Continuous (ductile, high V), Built-up edge (BUE, medium V), Discontinuous (brittle, low V)
questions.solution: Continuous: good finish. BUE: poor finish, built-up edge periodic. Discontinuous: chip breaks.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Polymer glass transition temperature (Tg) marks:
options: Transition from glassy to rubbery state
questions.solution: Tg: amorphous polymers change from hard/brittle to soft/flexible.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two springs of stiffness k each are connected in series. The combined stiffness is:
questions.solution: Series springs: 1/k_eq = 1/k + 1/k ⇒ k_eq = k/2, so the combination is softer.

questions.solution: η_Otto = 1 − 1/r^(γ−1); efficiency rises with compression ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Milling — up vs down:
options: Up (conventional): cutter opposite feed, safer, no backlash. Down (climb): better finish, needs backlash eliminator
questions.solution: Climb milling: chip thick at entry. Conventional: chip thin at entry. Climb preferred when possible.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydraulic gradient line (HGL) represents:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Merchant's circle — cutting force:
options: F_c = F_s·cos(β-α)/cos(φ+β-α). Shear angle φ controls chip thickness
questions.solution: Merchant: F_c (cutting), F_t (thrust). φ = 45° + α/2 - β/2. Shear strain: γ = cotφ + tan(φ-α).

questions.solution: Adiabatic means Q = 0, but expansion/compression changes temperature and internal energy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Resilience and toughness differ because resilience is energy absorbed ____ whereas toughness is energy absorbed ____:
options: Up to fracture; up to the elastic limit
Up to the elastic limit; up to fracture
questions.solution: Resilience = elastic strain energy; toughness = total energy absorbed up to fracture.

questions.stem: On the tensile stress–strain curve shown for mild steel (yield ≈ 350 MPa), point A (end of the initial straight line) represents the:
questions.solution: Point A is the yield point where the linear elastic region ends; the peak (B) is the ultimate strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stress concentration factor K_t:
options: K_t = σ_max / σ_nom. Geometric discontinuity (hole, notch, fillet)
questions.solution: K_t > 1. Fatigue: high SCF leads to crack initiation. Peterson's charts for K_t.

questions.stem: Composite beams:
options: Transformed section method. Width scaled by E_2/E_1. Same strain at interface
questions.solution: Bimetallic strip: different E values. Neutral axis shifts toward stiffer material.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The gauge pressure at depth h in a liquid of density ρ is:
questions.solution: Hydrostatic pressure p = ρgh increases linearly with depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure angle in a cam:
options: Angle between the normal to the pitch curve and the follower velocity
questions.solution: Pressure angle affects side thrust. Lower pressure angle = better force transmission.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A heat exchanger with parallel flow has:
options: Both fluids enter at the same end and flow in the same direction
questions.solution: Parallel flow: hot and cold enter same side. Counterflow: opposite ends (more efficient).
